description: OpenAI is the company behind ChatGPT and the GPT-4 API. KeyForge needs an API key so it can verify the credential is live and track usage. The key never leaves your KeyForge instance in plaintext.

suggested_scopes.label: Restricted (only the resources you pick in OpenAI)

steps.title: Open the OpenAI API keys page
steps.description: Sign in to your OpenAI account and go to the API keys page. If you have multiple projects, switch to the project you want this key to belong to first.

steps.title: Click Create new secret key
steps.description: Pick a descriptive name like KeyForge so you can recognise the key later. Leaving Project set to your default is fine.

steps.title: Pick the permissions
steps.description: All is the default and works everywhere. If you only plan to use a subset of OpenAI features, choose Restricted and pick just those resources. You can change this later by creating a new key.

steps.title: Copy the key and paste it below
steps.description: OpenAI shows the full key only once. Copy it now (it starts with sk- or sk-proj-) and paste it below. KeyForge encrypts it at rest the moment you click Save.
